signals.title: GBP/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: GBP/JPY at 215.815 — G10 leader-to-laggard spread reaches 1.389% as GBP outperformance (+0.734%) diverges from JPY weakness (-0.655%). BoE 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0% provides a +275bp carry premium, sustaining the bid despite JPY's positive CESI (+13). GBP strength persists despite soft US data as the SONIA-TONA spread remains the dominant driver in a normal volatility regime (VIX 16.0).

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Vol Premium
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.44 — Price holds above the 160.00 psychological threshold where BoJ verbal warnings typically intensify. Options market is pricing significant tail risk with a +5.0pt Vol Risk Premium (IV 7.2% vs HV30 2.2%) and a -1.24 25d RR put skew, signaling institutional demand for JPY protection despite the +275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage. Retail is extremely short (88%), creating a contrarian squeeze risk if 163.00 is tested.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5680 — COT LF net short -23,314 (specs fading the CB) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the 8 July meeting. Retail is 89% long, aligning with the hawkish OIS bias but vulnerable to a flush if the RBNZ fails to deliver. Real rate differential (-0.83% NZD vs +1.49% USD) remains a structural headwind for the kiwi despite the speculative short-squeeze potential.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Technicals
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4207 — Pair stalls below triple-top resistance as the correlation with WTI Oil breaks significantly (corr -0.833 vs -0.032 norm, z=-1.94σ). Despite WTI falling to $68.2 (-1.93%), CAD is holding its 1W average (+0.027%) as solid Q2 rebound data offsets energy weakness. COT LF net short -87,096 (long% 20.6%) reflects extreme bearishness that is failing to push price through resistance.

signals.title: EUR/USD — CESI Divergence + Vol Premium
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1384 — Strong negative CESI (-14) and ECB Kassik's suggestion of only one more hike weigh on the pair. Options market shows elevated hedging demand with a +2.5pt VRP (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.6%) as the Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.25% (+150bp) carry advantage favors USD. Watch for a break of 1.1350 to confirm the resumption of the 1M bearish trend (-2.27%).
